steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: “Food writer J. Kenji López-Alt, who explores the science behind cooking, ______ that lower boiling points …” The subject is the singular noun phrase Food writer J. Kenji López-Alt. The relative clause who explores the science behind cooking adds description but does not change the subject. The blank needs a singular, present-tense verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'explain': A plural present-tense form. It does not agree with the singular subject J. Kenji López-Alt. ✗
  • Choice B — 'explains': A singular present-tense form that agrees with the singular subject. The sentence reads naturally: “López-Alt … explains that …” ✓
  • Choice C — 'have explained': A plural present-perfect form. It fails both in number (plural) and in tense — the sentence describes an ongoing, general claim, not a completed past action. ✗
  • Choice D — 'are explaining': A plural present-progressive form. Again fails in number with the singular subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
